title: Show coverage rules compatible over the kind model
description:
Demonstrate that the existing coverage rules are compatible with the kind model without rewriting any live rule. Show that `label-coverage` evaluates identically whether its triple is expressed inline or referenced via a `kind=` key, and that the typed `criteria-to-check`/`criteria-label-match` rules consume the same `(section, marker, id-pattern)` triple a declared kind expands to (compatibility shown, not re-expressed), preserving the planning-bracket semantics they back. No live rule is modified.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: A `label-coverage` rule with `kind = "requirement"` produces findings identical to its inline `(section, marker, id-pattern)` form.
- [hard] REQ-02: A test asserts the inline triple of a `criteria-to-check` (or `criteria-label-match`) rule equals the declared kind's expansion.
- [hard] REQ-03: No live rule under `.jit/` or `docs/examples/` is modified.
- [hard] REQ-04: The compatibility assertions pass under `cargo test`.
